tab.placeholderInfo: You can use placeholder everywhere inside your pass by using ${Name}. The pass holder will be requested to fill in the placeholders when creating a pass if they do not have a default value. You can also use some predefined placeholders, see below list.

tab.redemptionRestrictionHelper: Stop issuing passes after specified amount of passes were redeemed

tab.passwordProtectionHelper: Only allow download of a pass using a password
tab.sharingRestrictionHelper:
Only allow one device to download and receive updates for a pass.
Please Note: This does not completely prevent pass sharing and may limit legitimate usage. Make sure you verify passes during redemption.
tab.returningUserHelper: Define what should happen if a user returns to the distribution URL after he has already created a pass.
tab.passSharingMethodHelper: Control the Wallet App Share Pass functionality on the back of the pass.

passTypes.title: Pass Type IDs
passTypes.renewMessage: Are you sure you want to renew this Pass Type Identifier? Apple support max 5 valid certificates per Pass Type Identifier. You don't need to renew if you have a valid certificate.
passTypes.deleteConfirmMessage: CAUTION: You are about to delete a pass type id. Without a pass type id, passes can't be signed and are not usable. Because of this all associated pass templates and passes will be permanently deleted from our servers.
passTypes.gettingStartedTitle: Getting started by adding a Pass Type ID.
passTypes.helperText: Register a pass type identifier (Pass Type ID) for each kind of pass you create (i.e. gift cards). Registering your Pass Type IDs lets you generate Apple-issued certificates which are used to digitally sign and send updates to your passes, and allow your passes to be recognized by Apple Wallet/Passbook.

assistedUpload.step1: Sign in with your Apple ID and go to
assistedUpload.step2: Register a new Pass Type ID if you don't have one
assistedUpload.step4: Locate the Pass Type ID in the Identifiers list and open it, then press Create Certificate
assistedUpload.step5: Upload your Certificate Signing Request to the Apple Developer Portal, then press Continue
assistedUpload.step6: Download your certificate from the Apple Developer Portal and upload it here.

sideCard.title: New Pass Type ID
sideCard.helperText: For production use, you are strongly encouraged to use your own Pass Type ID. You need an iOS Developer Account.

edit.title: Edit Pass Type ID
edit.infoText: The organization name will be updated in your Pass Type ID. The current organization name is
edit.warningText: The new organization name will be used for newly created passes. Passes issued with the old organization name might continue to use the old organization name even after being updated. You should not frequently change your organization name.

delete.confirmText: Please type {{passTypeIdentifier}} to confirm the deletion.
delete.warningText: All associated templates and passes will be permanently deleted!

import.certificateHelper: You can create your pass type manually, export and upload the p12 file here.

wizard.passTypeIdHelper: We recommend using a reverse-domain name style string (i.e., pass.dog.loyalty.merchant).
wizard.certificateNameHelper: You cannot use special characters such as @, &, *, ', ", -.
